title: Blueprint Phase Plan Model
description: Strict model-authored payload for phase.plan. Runtime-owned phase identity, plan id, phase directory, canonical filename, output path, and final Markdown persistence are supplied by MCP tools. The model renders Goal, Scope, Tasks, External Service Prerequisites, Verification, Must Haves, Requirement Coverage, Evidence Coverage, File / Surface Coverage, and Unknowns And Deferrals.

title.description: Human-readable plan title without the phase number or plan id; MCP renders the canonical filename and heading.

wave.description: Execution wave for this plan. Dependencies must be in earlier waves; the writer verifies the final saved plan set.

status.description: Phase plans are authored before execution, so the only valid model status is planned.

gapClosure.description: Optional true value renders gap_closure: true frontmatter for explicit gap-repair plans.

dependsOn.description: Existing plan ids this plan depends on. The task schema narrows this to saved plan ids for the selected phase and excludes the target plan id.

requirements.description: Roadmap requirement ids this specific plan covers now. This is a covered-now subset; do not include deferred or irrelevant requirements here. The task schema narrows values to the selected phase's known roadmap requirements, while requirementCoverage accounts for every known phase requirement exactly once.

filesModified.description: Concrete repo-relative files or generated Blueprint artifact paths this plan owns.

readFirst.description: Concrete repo-relative files or saved artifacts the executor must read before editing.

autonomous.description: Whether the plan can be executed without further human input after persistence.

scope.description: Exact work owned by this plan, rendered under ## Scope.

tasks.description: Concrete execution tasks. Each task id must be stable because coverage ledgers reference it.

externalServicePrerequisites.description: External services or runtime dependencies the agent cannot safely assume are ready. Keep this generic across project types: examples include container runtimes, databases, queues, emulators, local API servers, search services, caches, brokers, auth sandboxes, and third-party SaaS test tenants. Use the exact empty array when no external services are required.

verification.description: Objective verification items rendered under ## Verification.

mustHaves.description: Goal-backward facts the plan cannot drop.

requirementCoverage.description: Complete coverage ledger for the selected phase's roadmap requirements. The task schema requires every known phase requirement exactly once, with status covered, deferred, or irrelevant. Requirements covered by this plan also appear in top-level requirements; deferred and irrelevant requirements do not.

evidenceCoverage.description: Complete coverage ledger for the runtime-narrowed saved evidence inventory. The task schema narrows artifact values and requires every currently known saved evidence artifact exactly once. This inventory is dynamic: after a successful plan write, saved plan files can become evidence artifacts for later plan slots, so agents must re-read authoring context before each validation/write.

fileSurfaceCoverage.description: Coverage decisions for every declared filesModified surface.

unknownsAndDeferrals.description: Known unknowns, deferrals, blockers, or a concrete no-open-items row.

nonEmptyString.description: Non-empty single-line string. CR/LF is rejected because model strings are rendered into YAML frontmatter, Markdown lists, or Markdown tables.

repoRelativePath.description: Concrete single-line repo-relative path. No CR/LF, absolute paths, home-relative paths, traversal, backslashes, or glob metacharacters.

canAgentProceedWithoutIt.description: False means execute-phase should treat the service as a blocking preflight prerequisite until readiness is confirmed.
